steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence says critics have paid little attention to Hannock's paintings, and that this may be because the paintings seem so ______. The next sentence explains: critics focus on art that is cryptic or overtly challenging. The blank must describe the quality of the paintings that makes critics overlook them — something that is the opposite of cryptic or challenging.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "innovative": Innovation is often exactly what contemporary critics seek out and discuss. If the paintings were innovative, critics would be more likely to comment on them, not less. This doesn't explain the lack of critical attention. ✗
  • "subversive": Subversive work tends to attract critical commentary precisely because it challenges norms — similar to what critics value (cryptic, challenging). This would make critics more interested, not less. ✗
  • "profound": Profound art is typically the kind critics engage with deeply. Calling the paintings profound doesn't explain why critics would ignore them. ✗
  • "accessible": Accessible means easy to understand and appreciate — the direct opposite of cryptic or challenging. If the paintings are accessible, they lack the quality critics seek, which explains why critics overlook them even though viewers enjoy them. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
